purpose: Catch recursive importance, meaning, lesson, and work frames that reuse the same abstraction as both premise and payoff.
templates: {thinSubject} is not about {thinObject}; it is about {becomingFrame}.
{thinSubject} is trying to tell a {biggerStory} about what it means to {emptyMotion}.
{thinSubject} asks us to {softAction} the part of us that already {knowingVerb}.

becomingFrame: becoming more aligned with the lesson
becoming more human in the process

rejectIf: sentence names the concrete reason the moment matters
sentence names the concrete work, owner, deadline, or output
positiveExamples: This moment matters because it invites us to rethink what matters.
The work ahead opens space for more meaningful work.
The lesson is not about work; it is about becoming more aligned with the lesson.
The system is trying to tell a bigger story about what it means to move forward.
The journey asks us to listen to the part of us that already knows.
negativeExamples: This deadline matters because the vendor contract expires on June 30.
The work ahead creates space for two additional wheelchair seats in the waiting room.
The lesson is not in the workbook; it is in chapter 4.
The system reports why request 42 failed validation.
notes: This is intentionally recursive: the abstraction reappears as the claimed payoff.
It belongs in semantic-thinness, not phrases, because the slots are productive.
